info: CDE Acquisitions LLC is proposing a 6-building, 5.1-million-square-foot data center campus on ~410 acres of undeveloped industrial-zoned land across the street from Parkland High School. The project is currently under review with multiple agencies, including PPL, PA Department of Environmental Protection, PA Department of Transportation, and the Lehigh County Authority.

name: Smithfield Gateway Project
address: Off Seven Bridge Rd and Independence Rd
info: A developer has proposed a multi-building project consisting of data center facilities, an electrical substation, a hotel, and restaurant uses on approximately 91.96 acres of land. A submitted sketch plan illustrates the anticipated future land development for Phases 1B, 2A, and 2B of the Smithfield Gateway project. The plan proposes two data center buildings of approximately 125,000 square feet each (approximately 500,000 square feet of gross floor area in total), a power substation, a 110-room hotel, and two standalone restaurant buildings. The project would be developed across parcels 16.8.2.7 and 16.8.2.7-2, as roughly depicted by the project's map boundary.

name: Project Phoenix
address: Adjacent to the upcoming Shippingport Power Station
info: Frontier Group of Companies is transforming the Bruce Mansfield Power Plant into the Shippingport Power Station, a 3.6GW natural gas power facility with a colocated data center, representing a $10 billion investment. TThe campus is planned to span roughly 1.8 million square feet across two to three buildings. According to a press release from The Frontier Group of Companies, the project is expected to create over 15,000 construction jobs, 340 new full-time jobs, and generate $6 billion in new economic activity in the region.

name: Bradford County / Klondike Data Center Project
info: A data center is planned for colocation with New Fortress Energy's Wyalusing Energy Center, an upcoming natural gas power facility. This facility will be located somewhere on a 335-acre site in Wyalusing Township, owned by New Fortress Energy/Klondike, which offers over 150 developable acres. Notably, this project operates under a unique architecture: the natural gas power facility will not be connected to the regional PJM electric grid, but will instead directly power on-site data center operations.

name: Limerick / Project Laurel Data Center
address: Across from Philadelphia Premium Outlets, near the Limerick Nuclear Power Plant
info: An undisclosed developer, MCD 7 LLC, has proposed a 1.4-million-square-foot hyperscale data center campus on approximately 192 acres in Limerick Township, across from the Philadelphia Premium Outlets and near the Limerick Generating Station, a nuclear power plant. The plan includes eight 85-foot-tall data center buildings (five at 154,000 square feet, and three at 202,000 square feet) along with two 35-foot-tall administrative buildings, power substations, and secured fencing. The future tenant has not been disclosed due to a non-disclosure agreement. PECO, a gas and utility company, has agreed to supply up to 750 megawatts of power. According to the sources below, each data center building would include 30–45 backup generators and a similar number of rooftop chillers, and would employ 15–20 workers across three shifts. Full buildout is expected to take three to five years, pending completion of the township’s conditional use, zoning, and land development approval processes. The project is projected to generate $10 million to $20 million in annual tax revenue once fully built, primarily benefiting the Spring-Ford School District, and must comply with strict local data center zoning requirements related to noise, setbacks, and woodland preservation.

name: Peach Bottom Data Center
address: On site of York 2 Energy Center
info: Peach Bottom will host a $5 billion data center project on the site of the York 2 Energy Center. Not much information has been made public about this proposal yet.

name: NorthPoint Spring Mountain Data Center
info: NorthPoint Development has purchased hundreds of acres of land for a proposed data center around Spring Mountain. No specific details have been released about the site; anticipate updates as more information is released.

name: Penn Forest Township Data Center
info: Mele Brothers Realty has expressed interest in using their ~750 acres of commerically-zoned land off Rt 903 and Maury Rd for data center development. As of early March 2026, no applications have been filed, and Penn Forest Township supervisors have approved a zoning ordinance for data centers, requiring developers to meet specific noise, height, setback, etc. conditions to receive project approval.

name: Tobyhanna Township / Blakeslee Data Center
address: Pennsylvania Glacial Till property along Caughbaugh Road
info: Tobyhanna Township supervisors approved a zoning overlay allowing a data center on a 520-acre area called Glacial Till/Tarheel Quarry. This land is currently zoned rural residential and located near the Lehigh River, conservation areas, and wetlands. The ordinance permits data centers while imposing requirements such as large setbacks, minimum acreage, a closed-loop water system, noise limits of 70 decibels, and additional regulatory approvals. The overlay was adopted to meet zoning obligations and avoid legal challenges, despite resident concerns.

name: Talen Energy Rezone Request for Possible Data Center in Montour County
address: Between the Talen Energy Montour Power Plant and the Montour Preserve
info: Talen Energy has requested that Montour County rezone roughly 870 (originally 1300 according to sources) acres of mostly agricultural land in Anthony Township from agricultural to industrial use, citing potential future development that could include a large data center campus with approximately 12-15 buildings. In November, the Montour County Planning Commission voted against recommending the rezoning after determining that Talen’s plans were unclear, following public opposition from hundreds of residents concerned about the loss of prime farmland and potential impacts to water and community health. As of early February 2026, the Montour County Board of Commissioners has denied the rezoning request. Since then, Talen issued a statement saying they look forward to continued communication, and will provide more information on their plans for this site. Due to this, this proposal is considered delayed instead of withdrawn.

name: Fezzik Aliquippa Data Center
info: Five 9s Digital is marketing a 86-acre Pennsylvania site designed for a large-scale, natural gas–powered data center campus with up to 496 MW of power and on-site substations. The site is preapproved for behind-the-meter natural gas, supports an initial 250,000 SF data center expandable to 500,000 SF+, and allows further expansion across the property.

name: Dorrance Township Data Center Zoning Ordinance Challenge
address: Corner of South Main Rd and Yeager Rd
info: Brewster Land Company, LLC is challenging Dorrance Township’s zoning ordinance, arguing that the version in effect before a September amendment unlawfully excluded data centers as a permitted land use under Pennsylvania law. The company filed a substantive validity challenge with the zoning hearing board but has not yet submitted a formal application to build its proposed six-building data center on a 155-acre, commercially zoned parcel. October testimony focused on whether data centers were permitted or could be considered a conditional use under prior ordinance language, with township officials stating they were not expressly allowed. The hearing is bifurcated, and if the board rejects the challenge, the company may appeal to county court.

name: Project Green Mountain
address: East side of Casey Highway, at former Stavola Quarry site
info: Green Mountain 6, LLC has proposed a data center campus on former Stavola Quarry land above the Casey Highway. This campus will include seven two-story data center buildings with footprints of 138,000 SF (each building 276,000 GSF) with accessory back-up generator yards, a PP&L-owned switchyard, a customer substation, and extended detention basins. Due to similarities between Archbald's Project Green Mountain and Project Gravity, it is suspected that it that Green Mountain 6 is affiliated with Western Hospitality Partners, although this has not yet been confirmed.

name: East Manchester Farmland to Data Center Initiative Interest
address: York Baker Farm, off Long Rd and Gravel Hill Rd
info: East Manchester Township officials are planning zoning revisions for a 300-acre farm, York Baker Farm, to allow for data center development. Interest in this site has been expressed as it sits under two high-voltage power lines, and its vicinity to Brunner Island Power Station, which is already appropriately zoned for industrial use. Officials are drafting a zoning orindance to allow for data center development on the site, with public meeting expected on Jan 20th. On April 8th, 2026, a zoning ordinance was passed designating this land and a adjacent parcel as a data center overlay district.

name: Conewago Township Data Center Land Considerations
address: Properties along Bull Road and Mill Creek Road
info: Conewago Township officials are considering two rural areas for potential data center development: an agricultural wedding venue closing in 2026 on the western edge of the township and the Rexroth farm properties along the eastern border with Newberry Township. The Rexroth holdings total 541 acres and run along Bowers Bridge Road. Another set of properties under consideration includes an almost 130-acre parcel fronting Bull Road and bordering Dover Township, and a separate nearly 95-acre parcel on Mill Creek Road. Township planners are evaluating whether a data center overlay zone would apply to one or more of these locations.

name: Briar Creek Township Data Center Zoning Overlay
address: On Shickshinny Valley Road, below the Hemlock Springs development
info: Briar Creek Township supervisors are proposing a zoning overlay to allow for data center development on a 231-acre site across four parcels on Shickshinny Valley Road. The proposed overlay would apply to a site currently zoned for agricultural use, but would allow for data center development if approved.

name: South Hanover Township Data Center Ordinance
address: Over Industrial zoning of Pennsy Supply
info: South Hanover Township is currently drafting a data center overlay/zoning ordinance to allow for data centers on the Pennsy Supply site, which is currently zoned industrial. The township is holding a public hearing at 161 Patriot Way on March 24th at 6:00pm to receive public comments concerning the proposed zoning text and map amendments.

name: Lower Mount Bethel Technology Center
address: Around the intersection of Gravel Hill Rd and Martins Creek Belvidere Highway
info: J.G. Petrucci Company and Peron Development are proposing a $5 billion data center campus on 450 acres at the intersection of Gravel Hill Rd and Martins Creek Belvidere Highway, across the road from the John M. Mauser Nature Education Trail System. Currently this land is zoned agricultural, which would require a rezone/zoning ordinance to proceed.

name: Sellersville / West Rockhill Township Data Center
address: 215 Cathill Rd, next to former Teva Pharmaceutical building
info: A developer presented rough plans for a 350,000-square-foot data center on approximately 40 acres of land next to the former Teva Pharmaceutical building in West Rockhill Township. The site is zoned for industrial use and is being marketed under a partnership between National Land Developers and Greener by Design. As of mid-April 2026, no formal application has been submitted to the township.

name: Iron City No. 1 Power Generator and Compute Center
info: Iron City is possibly developing a data center at the Iron City No. 1 Power Generator and Compute Center, somewhere in the Karthaus area. The PA Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) approved Iron City Wells, LLC to construction 15 natural gas–fired engine generators to "power a compute facility", which still does not have much information public.

name: North Coventry Township Data Center
info: Envision Land Use withdrew plans for a small "boutique" data center along Route 100 at 299 W. Schuylkill Road after the North Coventry Township Board of Supervisors voted to send Envision a note expressing disinterest in a data center at the proposed location, prior to a formal application being submitted. This proposal included a 120,000-square-foot, three story data center building that would employ six full-time workers.

name: Prologis Warehouse Data Center
companies: Prologis; Allen Township Board of Supervisors; Lehigh Valley Planning Commission
info: A data center project is planned at 2500 Liberty Drive in Allen Township, Northampton County, Pennsylvania. The site has a 1,011,808 square foot warehouse owned by Prologis, an industrial real estate investment trust. The building was approved for warehouse use in 2016 and completed in 2023, but never occupied as a warehouse. Prologis sought and received unanimous approval from the Allen Township Board of Supervisors on March 24, 2026 to convert it to a data center. The end tenant is undisclosed. Plans are in early phases, but the township meeting cited that the facility will use a closed-loop cooling system, has a privately funded electrical infrastructure including a private substation, and will incorporate solar panels. No capacity figures were disclosed publicly. They further cite that the project is expected to employ between 100 and 120 people.

name: Former Pitt Race Site Data Center
info: Provident Data Centers has purchased the former Pittsburgh International Race Complex ("Pitt Race") for $50 million to develop data centers. Owners Jim and Kathy Stout announced the sale of the over 400-acre site in October 2025, and the beloved racing facility closed its doors a month later.

name: Allenwood Data Center
info: PNK Group is proposing a 300 MW 4-building data center campus on land they own and developed at the Great Stream Commons. On site, a 69-kilovolt electric substation is also proposed to be built to support the data centers. Before this proposal can move forward, Gregg Township would need to amend its zoning ordinance to add a permittance to data centers in commercial and manufacturing zones.

name: Fort Cherry Development District
companies: Imperial Land Corp.; Liberty Energy Inc.; Imperial Land Corp.; Liberty Energy Inc.; Range Resources Corp.
info: Imperial Land Corp. is developing the Fort Cherry Development District, an 875-acre industrial and office park, with initial phases encompassing 2.3 million square feet of industrial, flex, and office space. The district makes direct use of the Southern Beltway (PA-576), providing quick access to Pittsburgh International Airport, the Shell Cracker Plant in Beaver County, and Interstate 79. As part of the district, a data center project announced in spring 2025 would be powered by a natural gas plant built and operated by Liberty Energy Inc., with fuel supplied by Range Resources Corp. Power would be delivered in 25 MW blocks up to a total capacity of 1 GW, with the overall data center footprint scaled to the number of clients at the site.

name: Starpointe Data Center Project
companies: Next Generation Land; Next Generation Land; Next Generation Land; Energy Transfer; Range Resources Corp.
info: Next Generation Land is developing an 800+ acre hyperscale data center campus at Starpointe Business Park. The site is fully zoned industrial with data centers permitted by right, with 120+ acres already cleared, graded, and pad-ready, with an active NPDES permit in place. Power would be supplied behind-the-meter by an on-site natural gas plant connected to Energy Transfer's Rover Pipeline, capable of delivering up to 3.5 GW of capacity, with natural gas sourced from Range Resources. The project is currently in the early stages of development.

name: Champion Data Center Project
companies: Next Generation Land; Next Generation Land; Next Generation Land; Next Generation Land; PJM Interconnection
info: Next Generation Land is developing Champion, a 1,000+ acre hyperscale data center campus at a former coal-refuse brownfield site with existing industrial zoning. The campus is supported by six nearby natural gas pipelines, two of which run on-property, with a potential capacity of approximately 5 GW. The site carries a proven 300+ MW PJM interconnection history, an active NPDES permit, multiple fiber routes, and a Keystone Opportunity Zone designation. The project is currently in the early stages of development.

name: Colossus
address: 3231 Paul R. Lowry Road, Frank C. Pidgeon Industrial Park
companies: CTC Property LLC; Phoenix Investors; xAI; Memphis Light Gas and Water (MLGW); Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA); City of Memphis; Memphis City Council; Tennessee Department of Environment and Conservation (TDEC)
info: The xAI Colossus supercomputer is located at 3231 Paul R. Lowry Road in South Memphis, Tennessee, in a 785,000 square foot former Electrolux manufacturing facility situated on over 217 acres. An affiliate of Phoenix Investors acquired the building in December 2023. By March 2024 the property was leased to xAI. They took over the factory with the goal of building the world's largest supercomputer, named Colossus, to fuel artificial intelligence, with more than a dozen generators steadily burning methane gas visible at the site as early as September 2024. In November 2024, TVA approved xAI's request for over 100 MW of grid power supplied through MLGW, upgrading from an initial grid connection of only 8 MW (local news asserts Musk has said the site needs 150 MW). xAI submitted a contract to purchase 13 acres of City of Memphis-owned land at 2685 Plant Road, located on the south side of the T.E. Maxson Wastewater Treatment Facility, through its affiliate CTC Property LLC, for $820,000. The proposed Colossus Water Recycling Plant will process municipal wastewater from the T.E. Maxson Wastewater Treatment Plant with a planned capacity of 10.4 million gallons per day. TVA and Nucor Steel have signed Letters of Intent to purchase water from the facility alongside xAI, with peak demand by all three facilities estimated at 12.5 million gallons per day.

name: Colossus 2
info: This project shares its history with Colossus, and is an expansion of the initial site, located at 5420 Tulane Road. The facility, previously a 1,000,000 square foot warehouse owned by Coliers and built in late 2023. MZX TECH LLC, an affiliate of xAI, purchased the site in February 2025 for about $80 million. Based on satellite imagery and analysis of the cooling equipment, SemiAnalysis estimated Colossus 2 is progressing toward 400 MW of critical IT capacity, with a hybrid cooling system of approximately 130 dry coolers and 135 adiabatic cooling units, producing an estimated annual water footprint of approximately 346 million gallons per year (roughly 0.9 million gallons per day). By April 2025, aerial imagery showed 35 gas turbines operating at a combined 422 MW without permits, which the Southern Environmental Law Center stated makes the facility likely the largest industrial emitter of NOx in Memphis, setting a good estimate for its capacity.

name: ExoticRidge Bitcoin Mine
info: ExoticRidge is proposing a cryptomining facility adjacent to a gas fractionation plant in Bulls Gap. Beacon Center is currently representing ExoticRidge in a federal lawsuit that seeks to repeal a Hawkins County ban on data centers and cryptocurrency mines from late 2025. Because of this, the proposal is marked as "delayed."

name: Gallatin Data Center Expansion
info: In 2020, Meta began constructing their Gallatin data center campus, which consists of 12 data center buildings (called "data halls") and two administrative buildings.

name: Neural Nexus Data Center
info: In December 2025, Legacy Power Capital announced "Neural Nexus," a a massive $9.4 billion 1,742,400-square-foot mixed-use tech district to-be located in downtown Memphis. Situated on two city blocks near FedExForum, it features a 125,000-square-foot cloud computing center for AI, along with residential units, a hotel, and retail space.

name: RadiusDC Nashville I / Trinity Hills Colocation Data Center
info: RadiusDC began constructing a data center in the Trinity Hills area in 2025, and is expected to open in 2026. This facility is small and only has a capacity of 12 MW. Marketed as the largest colocation facility in Nashville.

name: 5C Memphis Data Center
info: 5C began constructing a data center campus in 2024 at the former Fred's headquarters. This represents a $152.1 million investment and has led the developer to seek a "15-year payment-in-lieu-of-taxes (pilot) with the Economic Development Growth Engine for Memphis and Shelby County (EDGE). The project is expected to be complete in 2027. IT load will be scaled across phases, starting at 15 MW, then 20 MW (2025), and finally 60 MW (2027).

name: Ridgeline Facility
address: Adjacent to the Tucker County Landfill

name: Adams Fork Harless Data Center Energy Campus, Holden
address: Harless Industrial Park, off 22 Mine Road
info: TransGas Development Systems, LLC is proposing a 2.4 GW, 2.8-million-square-foot data center campus in the Harless Industrial Park near Holden as part of the broader Adams Fork Energy Project. The facility is designed as an off-grid data center powered by approximately 2.4 GW of onsite, dispatchable generation, scalable to 9.6 GW. Power would be supplied by a dedicated microgrid natural-gas plant consisting of methane-fired engines with diesel backup fuel stored onsite. The campus is planned to use modular high-reliability infrastructure, dual-fuel capability, and geothermal-assisted cooling supported by a large underlying freshwater reserve. First power is projected within approximately 24 months.

name: Adams Fork Data Center Energy Campus, Wharncliffe
info: TransGas Development Systems, LLC is proposing a second off-grid data center campus near the Twisted Gun Golf Course in Wharncliffe as part of the Adams Fork Energy Project. This site would be powered by a dedicated onsite microgrid natural-gas generation plant composed of approximately 117 methane-fueled engines, with most units operating continuously and diesel backup available from onsite storage tanks if gas supply is interrupted.

name: Monarch Compute Campus and Mountaineer Gigasystem
address: Along W.Va 62, the Ohio River, and CSX Railroad tracks. Around Airport Rd and Hwy 6.
info: Fidelis New Energy is proposing a 2 GW, 2.25-million-square-foot data center campus along the Ohio River, north of Point Pleasant.

name: QTS Kearneysville Data Center Complex
address: Between W.Va Route 115 and CSX Railroad
info: QTS has proposed a new data center campus on a 300-acre site spanning Jefferson and Berkeley Counties, consisting of two Berkeley County parcels for two data center buildings and related infrastructure plus access from Route 115, and a 191-acre industrial-zoned parcel in Jefferson County. Berkeley County has approved the concept plan covering the two buildings, utility structures, and stormwater facilities, while Jefferson County has not yet received development plans. The property was acquired in July 2024, has access to major power and gas infrastructure, will initially use Berkeley County water and sewer service, and remains in the early planning stage according to QTS.

name: Penzance Intelligence Center
info: Penzance Management is proposing a data center on 548 acres of land around the Falling Waters and Bedington areas in Berkeley known as the Bedington Campus. The $4 billion privately funded project would total about 1.9 million square feet at completion, and would deliver at least 600 MW of critical IT capacity as the state’s first designated “High Impact Intelligence Center.” The development is expected to generate roughly 1,000 temporary construction jobs and at least 125 permanent full-time positions, with no direct state funding involved. The campus will likely be grid-connected, with Penzance preparing the site and probably partnering with hyperscale tenants that will ultimately operate within the facility.

name: Google Buffalo Data Center
info: The West Virginia Governor announced on March 27th that Google purchased 1700 acres of land directly north of Buffalo, WV. The plot was previously owned mostly by Franklin Real Estate and was an inactive farm at the time of purchase.

name: Martindale Indianapolis Metrobloks Data Center
info: Metrobloks is proposing a $500 million, 154,000-square-foot data center on a ~14 acre site in the Martindale-Brightwood neighborhood, at the former Sherman Drive-In site. This data center facility will offer 72 MW of IT capacity and will use a closed-loop cooling system. Metropolitan Development Commission approved the rezoning 6-2 in April 2026, despite strong community opposition. The recommendation heads to the City-County Council for final approval, though the district councilor has publicly supported the project.

name: Surge Development Knightstown Data Center
address: Near intersection of State Road 109 and Interstate 70
info: Surge Development LLC and GM Development LLC are developing a hyperscale data center on ~585 acres of land near the intersection of Interstate 70 and State Road 109. In January 2026, Henry County Commissioners voted 2-1 to approve the Planned Unit Development outline. No specific tenant has been publicized. This is the second development in Henry County that Surge has been approved for.

name: Decatur Technology Park
address: Intersection of Kentucky Avenue and Camby Road
info: Sabey Data Centers is proposing a 250 MW data center campus comprised of two 900,000-square-foot buildings on 130 acres of land near the intersection of Kentucky Avenue and Camby Road. This project represents an investment of up to $4 billion.Groundbreaking is expected for 2026, with phased opening through 2028-2030.

name: Project Louie
address: Bounded by Keller Hill Road, Antioch Road, State Road 42, and West Union Church Road
info: Google is proposing "Project Louie", a hyperscale data center campus just east of Monrovia in Monroe Township, bounded roughly by Keller Hill Road (north), Antioch Road (east), State Road 42 (south), and West Union Church Road (west). County commissioners originally rezoned approximately 391 acres for the project in February, later approving the rezoning of an additional 158 acres, and Google separately purchased a 79-acre farm within Monrovia's town limits, bringing its total land holdings to approximately 628 acres. The project drew significant public opposition over energy and water demands and the use of nondisclosure agreements among county officials, though local leadership was ultimately more receptive than those in Franklin Township (withdrawn Project Flo), approving the rezoning and development agreement.

name: Hancock County MegaSite
info: Surge Development withdrew their proposal for a 775-acre data center campus, referred to as the Hancock County MegaSite, near Tuttle Orchards.

name: Project Flo
address: North CR 400 West at West CR 500 North
info: Google withdrew its proposal "Project Flo", a $1 billion, 468-acre data center campus on the southeast side of Indianapolis. Google negotiated through a holding company called Deep Meadow Ventures LLC, keeping their identity hidden behind non-disclosure agreements while they sought to rezone agricultural and residential land along I-74 and Post Road into a commercial special district to house four data centers. The project was expected to take 5-10 years to complete, and was expected to create 350 full-time jobs after completion. The proposal faced fierce opposition, leading Google to abruptly withdraw the proposal, minutes before the Indianapolis City-County Council was set to vote. This is possibly one of the first successful citizen campaigns against a major AI data center project in the United States.

name: Pike Township Data Center
address: Near West 79th Street and Township Line Road
info: American Tower Corporation has withdrawn their plans for a 4,400-square-foot data center near 79th Street and Township Line Road.

name: South Annville Township Data Center
address: Along Mt. Pleasant Road, adjacent to 911 Rapid Response
companies: Inch & Co; Eastern Land & Resources Co
info: Inch & Co. and Eastern Land & Resources Co. are proposing a $1.7 billion data center on 99 acres adjacent to the 911 Rapid Response building in South Annville. The developer has stated that the proposal is still in the early conceptual stage, so exact capacity and water usage details are not yet known, however, five buildings are expected to be constructed, and they would emit between 45-55 decibels. This proposal also sits adjacent to a much larger industrial complex, DHL's Clear Spring Logistics Park, which will house multiple warehouses. The property is zoned I-1 (industrial) and C-1 (commercial), and the township has set out three requirements for the project. Among them, the conversion of 15 industrial acres to agricultural use, both to protect an eagle nest in the southwest corner of the property and to offset 13 acres being rezoned from agricultural to industrial. Now, next steps include securing approvals through the township’s planning commission, zoning, and township supervisors. It is unclear if the project would continue if the rezone request was denied.

name: Newton Township Data Center
address: East Newton Ira Tripp Tract / E Newton IRA Trip Township Road L R
info: Popca LLC is proposing a $280 million, two-building data center campus on roughly 90 acres in across the road from another proposed data center in Ransom Township. Each building would stand 35 feet tall and span 145,200 square feet, with supporting infrastructure including generation yards, a substation, well, septic system, guard building, and stormwater facilities. The township's zoning officer denied the application in February, ruling that data centers are not a permitted use in Newton's rural resource zoning district. Popca LLC has since appealed, with their attorneys arguing the denial misapplies the zoning ordinance and that Pennsylvania law requires municipalities to zone for all possible uses. The appeal hearing is scheduled for April 27 at 7 p.m. at the Newton Township municipal building in Clarks Summit.

name: East Greenwich Township Data Center
info: American Tower is proposing an 17,000-square-foot “unmanned data storage center” on residentially zoned land along Mantua Road, adjacent to the Oak Ridge housing development.

name: CoreWeave Kenilworth
info: CoreWeave is constructing a $1.8 billion AI data center across 392,600 square feet at the former Merck & Co. campus. This project was the first granted a $250 million tax credit award under the Next New Jersey Program – AI (NJEDA), which is aimed at drawing significant capital investment, creating high-paying jobs and cementing New Jersey’s position as a leader in the AI industry economy, according to the EDA. Construction began in late 2025, and operation is anticipated in early 2027.

name: Project Nebius
info: Nebius Group is redeveloping the former Regions Bank operations campus on Lakeshore Parkway into a 300 MW AI computing facility spanning roughly ~75–80 acres. Filed under permit BHM01 in January 2026 and marketed as the "Birmingham AI Factory," the project will demolish existing structures for a purpose-built GPU data center. It faces community opposition over power demand, noise, and proximity to residential neighborhoods, and is proceeding under pre-moratorium development rights while a city-wide pause on new large data centers is in effect.

name: Project Marvel
address: Off Rock Mountain Lake Road, near the I-459/I-20/59 interchange
info: Logistics Land Investment LLC is proposing an 18-building, 4.5 million sq ft hyperscale complex targeting 1.2 GW of power capacity. Originally planned on ~700 acres rezoned from agricultural to light industrial in November 2025, the project expanded in early 2026 to a revised master plan totaling approximately 1,600 acres. The $14.5 billion project has drawn sustained community opposition over environmental impact, energy use, and transparency concerns, and multiple rezoning and planning steps remain before construction can proceed.

name: Meta Platforms Data Center
address: Off I‑65 near the Hyundai plant
info: Meta is constructing a massive 715,000 sq ft AI data center off I-65, near the Hyundai assembly plant, representing a $800 million investment. Initially announced in May 2024 as an $800 million, 715,000 sq ft facility, the project was subsequently expanded, bringing the total investment to $1.5 billion and the campus to over 1.3 million sq ft. Powered entirely by renewable energy and targeting LEED Gold certification, the facility is expected to come online by the end of 2026 and will support approximately 100 permanent jobs.

name: AUBix + Core Scientific Data Center
info: Core Scientific is investing $400 million to expand its Auburn data center, which will be hosted within the existing AUBix site. At launch the data center will have a capacity of 16 MW.

name: Clinton Amazon Data Center
info: The Clinton project, announced in April 2026, retrofits the former Delphi Corporation plant and represents Amazon's first location in the city, with a planned investment of $1 billion and the creation of 100 jobs. Amazon has mentioned that the Clinton facility will use no water for cooling operations. Hinds County and the city of Clinton are both assisting with the project's incentives.

name: Ridgeland Amazon Data Center
info: Governor Tate Reeves announced in April 2026 that Amazon will invest an additional $11 billion to expand its existing data center operations in Madison County, with one of the new facilities located in Ridgeland. The Ridgeland site is located on the Costas Property, west of Tougaloo College near County Line Road. The Ridgeland campus is expected to use groundwater only about 30 days of the year, or roughly 9% of the time.

name: Canton Amazon Data Center
info: Amazon Web Services is investing $10 billion to build a hyperscale data center complex outside of Canton at the Madison County Mega Site, spanning part of roughly 1,700 acres across two sites. Amazon broke ground on this campus two years ago, and it was the largest capital investment in Mississippi's history at the time. Amazon has pledged to use 100% recycled water to cool the servers at its Canton data centers.

name: Vicksburg Amazon Data Center
info: Amazon announced in November 2025 a $3 billion AI data center off U.S. 61 South Warren, with construction set to begin in 2026, along with a $150,000 educational grant for Warren County. The facility is expected to create at least 200 high-paying full-time jobs and falls under Mississippi's 2024 Major Economic Impact Authority legislation that has supported Amazon's broader data center expansion in the state.

name: Project Taurus
info: AVAIO Digital Partners plans a $6 billion “Taurus” data center campus on 175 acres in the East Metropolitan Center Business Park. Announced in Aug 2025, the AI-ready campus will support cloud and AI workloads with sustainable design (rainwater capture, solar, efficient cooling). The project will be powered by Entergy and have multi-hundred-megawatt capacity (~450 MW online by 2027). Initial phases will create about 60 direct high-tech jobs (plus hundreds of construction and support jobs).

name: Clarksdale Data Center
info: In March 2026, Clarksdale officials voted to approve a rezone with conditions to allow for a data center on a 648-acre site. The developer would be required to pay one-time $5-million impact fee prior to any work.

name: xAI MACROHARDRR
info: xAI is investing over $20 billion to build a major data center in Southaven, Mississippi, known as MACROHARDRR, a playful jab at rival Microsoft. The 800,000-square-foot facility sits in DeSoto County adjacent to the Tennessee border, forming part of a tri-center AI cluster that xAI CFO Anthony Armstrong described as housing "the world's largest supercomputer" with a combined 2 gigawatts of computing power. Governor Tate Reeves called it the largest private investment in the state's history, with the project expected to bring hundreds of permanent jobs and significant tax revenue to DeSoto County. Despite the enthusiasm, the NAACP and the Southern Environmental Law Center have raised concerns over air pollution near predominantly Black communities in Memphis, and a local advocacy group submitted a petition citing concerns about noise and emissions from the gas turbines powering the site.

name: New Castle Data Center
address: By I-81 on the south and West Laurel Street on the north
info: KRNL Data Centers approached New Castle Township officials about rezoning an 89-acre conservation tract near Frackville to allow for data center construction. The property is currently owned by Mount Laurel Enterprises and sits behind Broad Mountain Health & Rehabilitation Center near Frackville's Laurel Street residential area. Though no formal plans have been filed, the proposed complex would reportedly consist of multiple buildings rising above the tree line. The decision on whether to approve the project would rests with New Castle Township supervisors.

name: Project West
address: West of Highway 28 and south of Delaware Street
info: Tract is developing a 282-acre site, representing an investment of $12 billion, west of Highway 28 and south of Delaware Street.

name: Applied Digital Adair
address: Adjacent to NextEra substation, 1200 block of Fallow Avenue

name: Project Alluvion
info: Project Alluvion is a data center campus owned by Microsoft composed of 8 buildings.

name: Project Osmium
info: Microsoft Project Osmium is a $2-billion multi-stage hyperscale data center campus located at 5855 SW Kerry St. This project was announced in 2016 and is a key infrastructure investment for Microsoft Azure, featuring five planned buildings totaling over 1.7 million square feet and over 150 MW capacity. Construction was anticipated to be completed in 2025.

name: Brookhaven National Laboratory Data Center
address: Adjacent to William Floyd Parkway, on Brookhaven National Labratory's campus
info: The U.S. Department of Energy is considering developing an AI data center on a 90-acre section of Brookhaven National Laboratory's campus, adjacent to the William Floyd Parkway. The proposed site is nearby the Caithness Long Island Energy Center, a 350 MW gas plant.
